pactoVersion.description: The version of the Pacto contract specification this file conforms to.

service.description: Identifies the service: its name, version, owner, and optional container image.

name.description: A unique, DNS-compatible identifier for the service (lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens).

version.description: The current version of the service, typically following semantic versioning.

owner.description: The team or individual responsible for this service.

image.description: Container image details for deploying the service.

ref.description: The full container image reference (e.g. ghcr.io/org/image:tag).

private.description: Whether the image is stored in a private registry requiring authentication.

interfaces.description: The network interfaces the service exposes. Each interface declares how external or internal consumers can communicate with the service.

name.description: A unique name for this interface within the service.

type.description: The communication protocol used by this interface.

x-enum-descriptions.http: serves HTTP/REST traffic and requires a port
x-enum-descriptions.grpc: serves gRPC traffic and requires both a port and a protobuf contract
x-enum-descriptions.event: produces or consumes asynchronous messages and requires an event contract

port.description: The TCP port the interface listens on (required for http and grpc types).

visibility.description: Controls who can reach this interface.

x-enum-descriptions.public: accessible from outside the cluster or network boundary
x-enum-descriptions.internal: only accessible within the cluster or internal network

contract.description: Path to the interface contract file (OpenAPI spec for http, protobuf for grpc, event schema for event).

configuration.description: Declares the service's configuration model via a JSON Schema file that describes expected environment variables or config entries.

schema.description: Path to a JSON Schema file describing the service's configuration properties.

dependencies.description: Other services this service depends on. Each dependency references another service's published Pacto contract.

ref.description: An OCI reference to the dependency's published Pacto contract (e.g. ghcr.io/org/service-pacto@sha256:...).

required.description: Whether this dependency is mandatory. If true, the service cannot function without it; if false, the service degrades gracefully when it is unavailable.

compatibility.description: A semver range expressing which versions of the dependency this service is compatible with (e.g. ^2.0.0, ~1.3.0).

runtime.description: Describes how the service behaves at runtime: its workload pattern, state management, lifecycle policies, and health checks.

workload.description: The execution pattern of the workload.

x-enum-descriptions.service: a long-running process that serves requests continuously
x-enum-descriptions.job: a one-shot task that runs to completion and then exits
x-enum-descriptions.scheduled: a task that runs on a recurring schedule (e.g. cron)

state.description: Declares how the service manages state, including whether it retains data, how that data is persisted, and how critical the data is.

type.description: Whether the service needs data from a previous request to handle the current one. A pure API proxy is stateless, an in-memory cache that can be rebuilt is stateless, but an API that stores user sessions in memory is hybrid.

x-enum-descriptions.stateless: does not retain data between requests; any instance can handle any request (e.g. an API proxy or a service whose in-memory data can be fully rebuilt from external sources)
x-enum-descriptions.stateful: retains data between requests and requires stable storage or affinity (e.g. a database, a message broker, or a service with on-disk state)
x-enum-descriptions.hybrid: handles requests statelessly but keeps selective in-memory or local state that enriches behaviour (e.g. an API with a local cache, in-memory session store, or background-refreshed data)

persistence.description: Describes the scope and durability of the service's persisted data.

scope.description: Whether data is confined to a single instance or shared across instances. In-memory state is always local; a database or shared cache is shared.

x-enum-descriptions.local: data is confined to a single instance and not shared across replicas (e.g. in-memory caches, local files, embedded databases)
x-enum-descriptions.shared: data is shared across all instances via a common store (e.g. a database, Redis, or shared filesystem)

durability.description: Whether the data survives process restarts. In-memory data is ephemeral by definition; data written to disk or an external store is persistent.

x-enum-descriptions.ephemeral: data can be lost on restart without impact; used for in-memory caches, temp files, or reconstructible state
x-enum-descriptions.persistent: data must survive restarts and be durably stored (e.g. on disk, in a database, or in an external store)

dataCriticality.description: The business impact of losing this service's data.

x-enum-descriptions.low: loss of data has minimal business impact; can be regenerated or is non-essential
x-enum-descriptions.medium: loss of data has moderate business impact and may require manual recovery
x-enum-descriptions.high: loss of data has severe business impact and must be prevented (e.g. financial records)

lifecycle.description: Optional lifecycle policies controlling how the service is upgraded and shut down.

upgradeStrategy.description: The strategy used when deploying a new version of the service.

x-enum-descriptions.rolling: new instances are brought up incrementally while old ones are drained, ensuring zero downtime
x-enum-descriptions.recreate: all existing instances are terminated before new ones are started; causes brief downtime
x-enum-descriptions.ordered: instances are updated one at a time in a fixed order, useful for stateful workloads with leader election

gracefulShutdownSeconds.description: The number of seconds the platform waits for the service to finish in-flight requests before forcefully terminating it.

health.description: Configures how the platform checks whether the service is healthy and ready to receive traffic.

interface.description: The name of the interface that serves the health check endpoint.

path.description: The HTTP path for the health check (e.g. /health or /readyz).

initialDelaySeconds.description: The number of seconds to wait after the container starts before sending the first health check probe.

scaling.description: Defines replica count as either an exact number or a min/max range.

replicas.description: An exact, fixed number of replicas. Mutually exclusive with min/max.

min.description: The minimum number of replicas that must always be running.

max.description: The maximum number of replicas the platform is allowed to scale up to.

metadata.description: A free-form object for attaching custom key-value metadata to the service contract.
